PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER SFS 39, INC. (DBA THE DEAD FISH, THE FRANCISCAN CRAB HOUSE, AND THE CRAB HOUSE) FRONCILLO, ANDREA DAL BOZZO, JERRY SERAFINI, DANTE AS TO RESPONDENT WILLIAMS, JENNIFER INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED WILLIAMS, JOHN INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED GADDIS, MARCELLA INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED BANUELOS, JOSE INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONERS SFS 39, INC., ANDREA FRONCILLO, JERRY DAL BOZZO AND DANTE SERAFINI'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATOR EXCEEDED HIS AUTHORITY IN ORDERING THE CLASS CLAIMS TO ARBITRATION BECAUSE THERE IS NOT A SUFFICIENT BASIS TO CONCLUDE THAT THE PARTIES AGREED TO AUTHORIZE CLASS ARBITRATION IN THEIR ARBITRATION AGREEMENTS. (KINECTA ALTERNATIVE FINANCIAL SOLUTIONS, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 506.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memo Of P & A; Appendix Of Foreign Authorities And Pos
Off Calendar Jul-09-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anthony J. Zaller, Esq., from Van Vleck, Turner & Zaller, LLP, 323-592-3505, for petitioner and moving party
Nicholas A. Carlin, Esq., from Phillips, Erlewine & Given, LLP, 415-398-0900, for respondent and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONERS SFS 39, INC., ANDREA FRONCILLO, JERRY DAL BOZZO AND DANTE SERAFINI'S motion To Vacate Arbitration Award is Granted. The arbitrator exceeded his authority in ordering the class claims to arbitration because there is not a sufficient basis to conclude that the parties agreed to authorize class arbitration in their arbitration agreements. (Kinecta Alternative Financial Solutions, Inc. v. Superior Court, (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 506.)
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976
